creator: mail@fbsd2.e4m.org
text:
> Do you have any ideas for improving the current situation?

Well, I played a bit with (WARNING: copy and paste patch):

--- usr.sbin/autofs/common.c.ORI      2023-12-27 17:50:13.000000000 +0100
+++ usr.sbin/autofs/common.c    2024-12-12 10:18:06.898456000 +0100
@@ -978,6 +978,8 @@
 
        error = auto_pclose(yyin);
        yyin = NULL;
+       if( WEXITSTATUS(error) == 222 )
+               exit( 1 );
        if (error != 0)
                log_errx(1, "failed to handle special map \"%s\"", map);
 
voyager:/usr/src>odiff usr.sbin/autofs/popen.c 
--- usr.sbin/autofs/popen.c.ORI 2023-12-27 17:50:13.000000000 +0100
+++ usr.sbin/autofs/popen.c     2024-12-12 10:13:37.316801000 +0100
@@ -179,7 +179,7 @@
        }
 
        if (WEXITSTATUS(status) != 0) {
-               log_warnx("\"%s\", pid %d, terminated with exit status %d",
+               (WEXITSTATUS(status) == 222  ? log_debugx : log_warnx)( "\"%s\", pid %d, terminated with exit status %d",
                    cur->command, pid, WEXITSTATUS(status));
                return (status);
        }


This allows things like:

--- special_hosts.ORI        2024-01-04 07:30:11.000000000 +0100
+++ special_hosts       2024-12-12 10:18:22.930250000 +0100
@@ -9,6 +9,10 @@
        exit 0
 fi
 
+
+! /usr/local/bin/fping -q -B 1 -r 30 -t 100 $1 && exit 222
+! /usr/local/bin/tcptest -nq $1 nfsd && exit 222
+
 out=`showmount -E "$1"`
 [ $? -eq 0 ] || exit 1
 echo "$out" | awk -v host="$1" \


(tcptest is a small programme which checks if port argv[2] is connectable on host [1])

Together with vfs.autofs.debug=0 it is silent now.

Instead of calling simply exit(1) in common.c we could do something like done(EIO, true) in automountd.c but with a different errno and make sys/fs/autofs/autofs.c not spit out the warning if ar->ar_error is not EIO.

> ... perhaps this bug can be closed as works as intended.

Well, while we can argue that with vfs.autofs.debug != 0 one has to tolerate the kernel messages, I personally don't think we should spam the user with daemon.warn from automountd.

I also think that vfs.autofs.retry_attempts != 0 is a good thing as default.

IMHO best thing would be if there was a way to

1. let automountd tell the kernel that a mount failed in a somewhat non-critical way (e.g. by returning something different instead of EIO or by whatever means).

2. let the kernel be quiet and ignore retries if 1. apply.

automountd could determine these non-critical failure by a somewhat automatic approach as above or by a simple config var (automountd_ignore_hosts="host1 host2"). Or one could simply add something like

if [ -x /some/user/script ]; then
  /some/user/script $1 || exit 222   (or whatever)
fi

and the user can implement whatever tests in there...
